# A Versus B

> A Versus B (aversusb.net) is the internet's most comprehensive structured comparison platform — helping users make informed decisions by comparing anything side-by-side across 17+ categories including sports, countries, technology, products, software, automotive, health, finance, and more.

*Last updated: July 11, 2026 (HB388)*

## What We Do

A Versus B provides data-driven, expert-reviewed comparisons with:
- Attribute-by-attribute comparison tables with per-attribute winners
- Short answer summaries (quick TL;DR for LLM citation)
- Verdict cards with a clear winner recommendation
- Key differences highlighted with context
- Pros and cons for each entity
- FAQPage schema markup with structured Q&A
- Community voting data (10,000s of user votes)
- Related comparisons and alternatives
- Visual bar/radar charts
- Video explainers (YouTube embeds)
- Citation statistics (source count, data point count)

Every comparison is structured for machine-readable extraction with Schema.org markup: Article, TechArticle, NewsArticle (for recent comparisons), FAQPage, HowTo (for product/tech decision guides), BreadcrumbList, ItemList, Dataset, AggregateRating, DataCatalog, VideoObject, ProfilePage, and Product (with brand, offers.availability).

## Scale

- **3,000+** comparison pages covering X vs Y topics
- **370+** blog articles covering tactical comparison questions
- **2,500+** entity pages with individual profiles
- **17+** major categories
- Updated continuously; data refreshed monthly

## Structured Data Coverage (Schema.org)

Every comparison page emits a full JSON-LD @graph including:
- `Article` / `TechArticle` (tech/software categories) / `NewsArticle` (recent comparisons < 180 days old in tech/sports/gaming/entertainment/automotive) — with headline, description, datePublished, dateModified, speakable, accessMode, `accessModeSufficient`, `accessibilityFeature` (tableOfContents, readingOrder, structuralNavigation, alternativeText, bookmarks), keywords, interactionStatistic, abstract, lastReviewed, `citation` (typed citation array: external sources as `WebPage` with domain publisher, Wikipedia references as `Article` with Wikipedia organization node — enables AI fact-checker provenance chains), `assesses` (evaluation framing phrase for AI routing), `significantLink` (entity ProfilePage + alternatives URLs), `potentialAction: ReadAction`, `interactivityType`, `isPartOf: WebSite @id` (graph merging), `review` (verdict as formal Review node when verdict present), `copyrightNotice`/`copyrightYear`/`copyrightHolder`/`acquireLicensePage` (AI training attribution), `audience` (topic-audience routing), `genre` (category-specific e.g. "Technology Comparison"), `thumbnailUrl` (direct OG image URL for AI visual crawlers), `image.contentUrl` (machine-readable image asset URL alongside url), `tableOfContents` (section list: Quick Answer · Key Differences · Side-by-Side Comparison · Verdict · FAQ)
- `HowTo` schema on product/tech/automotive/gaming/software comparisons with ≥3 attributes — step-by-step guide for "how to choose X vs Y" queries with per-attribute winner steps and verdict
- `FAQPage` with up to 10 structured Q&A pairs
- `BreadcrumbList` for navigation hierarchy
- `ItemList` for the compared entities with position ordering
- `Dataset` with variableMeasured attributes and isBasedOn citation sources
- `AggregateRating` when community vote threshold is met (10+ votes)
- `SpeakableSpecification` targeting #short-answer (Quick Answer TL;DR box), #verdict, #key-differences, #key-facts, **#faq** (FAQ section with Q&A pairs) — all sections now have real DOM id anchors (HB88); FAQPage also emits its own speakable targeting `.faq-answer` elements so AI models extract answers directly
- `VideoObject` when a comparison video exists (YouTube or self-hosted)
- `DataCatalog` site-wide — positions the entire site as a structured comparison database
- `DefinedTermSet` on hub pages — topic glossary/directory signals
- `CollectionPage` on category, subcategory, hub, blog listing, review listing, trending, and studies index pages — all with `@id`, `genre` (category-appropriate, e.g. "Category Index", "Topic Hub", "Review Index", "Trending Index", "Research Hub"), `thumbnailUrl`, `image.contentUrl`, `contentReferenceTime`, `interactivityType: "expositive"`, `isPartOf: WebSite @id`, `potentialAction: ReadAction`, `alternativeHeadline`, `license` (CC-BY-4.0), `usageInfo`, `accessMode`, `accessModeSufficient`, `accessibilityFeature` (HB130)
- `ProfilePage` on entity pages — with `@id` anchor, `genre: "Entity Profile"`, `thumbnailUrl` (direct OG image URL), `contentReferenceTime`, `interactivityType: "expositive"`, `alternativeHeadline`, `license`, `usageInfo`, `accessMode`, `accessModeSufficient`, `significantLink`, `primaryImageOfPage` (ImageObject for Knowledge Panel and AI Overview thumbnail slot), `copyrightNotice`, `copyrightHolder`, `acquireLicensePage`, `audience`, `keywords` (`{entity} comparison, {entity} vs, best {entity} alternatives 2026`), top-level `potentialAction: ReadAction`, `citation` array (top-5 related comparison pages as `WebPage` objects — enables AI entity attribution chains)
- `ProfilePage` on author page `/authors/daniel-rozin` — with `@id: #profilepage`, `genre: "Author Profile"`, `thumbnailUrl`, `image.contentUrl`, `contentReferenceTime`, `interactivityType: "expositive"`, `datePublished`/`dateModified`, `isPartOf: WebSite @id`, `potentialAction: ReadAction`, and linked `Person` node with `knowsAbout`, `sameAs`, `publishedWork`
- `Product` enrichment on product-type entities — `brand`, `offers.availability: InStock`, `category`, `priceCurrency` on comparison `about` nodes, @graph itemNodes, profilePageSchema mainEntity, and entityPageSchema
- `SearchResultsPage` on the search results view
- `ImageObject` with contentUrl, name, description, caption on OG images
- `ContactPage` on the contact page with embedded `ContactPoint` organization node
- `WebPage` + `BreadcrumbList` on requests and site-map pages for full crawl coverage
- Citation meta (`citation_title`, `citation_author`, `citation_abstract`, `DC.*`) on all page types for academic and LLM indexers
- `copyrightNotice`, `copyrightHolder`, `acquireLicensePage`, `audience` on ALL page types (100% coverage: comparisons, blog, entity, category, subcategory, hub, alternatives, reviews, studies, FAQ, about, trending, best-of, curated guides, and more)
- Blog `Article` enriched with full `ImageObject` (OG image 1200×630 with creditText, creator, copyrightHolder) for rich result thumbnail eligibility; `citation` array (related comparison pages as `WebPage` objects — LLM trust signal for claim verification); `mentions` array (article tags mapped to `Thing` entities for knowledge-graph co-occurrence linking)
- `isPartOf: WebSite @id` on comparison and blog Article schemas for knowledge graph entity merging
- `contentReferenceTime` on all comparison and blog Article schemas — tells LLMs the data freshness window for time-qualified answers
- `og:site_name` + `og:locale` on comparison pages for correct publisher attribution in social unfurlers and AI scrapers
- `twitter:site @aversusb` on all page types — comparison, blog, entity, category, reviews, alternatives (publisher handle for X/Twitter card richness)
- `identifier` on DataCatalog — stable citation ID for Google Dataset Search and AI research tools
- `mainEntity: DataCatalog` on WebSite schema — direct topic link for Google Knowledge Graph and AI crawlers
- `founder` on Organization (Daniel Rozin, with LinkedIn sameAs) — E-E-A-T signal connecting Organization to a named expert
- `socialSameAs` now always emits X (twitter.com/aversusb), LinkedIn, and YouTube — even without env vars
- `genre: "Comparative Analysis"` + `educationalLevel: "General"` + `interactivityType` on multi-entity (3+) Article schemas (parity with 2-entity)
- `lastReviewed` + `reviewedBy` on multi-entity Article schemas for freshness verification
- `genre/educationalLevel/wordCount/articleSection` on blog `hasPart` WebPage nodes
- `teaches` (e.g. "How to choose between Slack and Teams") + `educationalUse: "comparison"` + `discussionUrl` (Reddit search) on all comparison Article schemas (both 2-entity and multi-entity) — routes LLM decision-intent queries to our pages
- `educationalUse` and dynamic `teaches` on blog Article schemas — "comparison" for vs-articles, "guide" for how-to articles
- `citation_keywords` meta tag (`;`-separated entity names + category) and `DC.subject` on comparison pages for academic/AI indexer classification; research/study pages also have `citation_keywords`, `citation_fulltext_world_readable`, `DC.subject`, `DC.rights`, `DC.coverage` for Semantic Scholar + Google Scholar open-access indexation
- `isBasedOn[]` on entity ProfilePage — Wikipedia `Article` + DBpedia `Dataset` provenance nodes; formal E-E-A-T chain for AI citation pipelines
- `mainEntityOfPage → ProfilePage` on entity nodes in comparison Article `about[]` (both 2-entity and multi-entity) — typed cross-document graph edge from Article → Entity → ProfilePage traversable in a single page parse
- `geographicArea: { "@type": "AdministrativeArea", name: "Worldwide" }` on every `Audience` node site-wide — signals global authority to Discover, Bing AI, Perplexity
- `potentialAction: SubscribeAction` on blog listing + trending CollectionPage — follow/subscribe intent signaling for AI Overview sidebar actions
- All `Audience` nodes carry `geographicArea`; all `accessibilityFeature` arrays carry the full 5-value set: `tableOfContents, structuralNavigation, alternativeText, readingOrder, bookmarks` (100% coverage)
- `isPartOf: Article` back-reference on FAQPage schema — formal FAQ→Article graph edge so AI systems attribute Q&A pairs correctly; `upvoteCount: 1` on each Answer node
- `image` (OG image ImageObject) and `url` (section anchor) on every `HowToStep` — enables Google Step Images rich result in SERP
- `estimatedCost: $0` on HowTo schema for free comparison tool signal
- `speakable` expanded on entity ProfilePage: `["h1", "#entity-intro", "#entity-about"]` — voice assistants now extract entity intro lede + curated description
- `id="entity-intro"` and `id="entity-about"` DOM anchors on entity page for SpeakableSpecification targeting
- `<link rel="preconnect">` for pagead2.googlesyndication.com + fonts.gstatic.com in Pages Router _document.tsx — reduces TCP handshake latency for LCP resources
- `@id` on `about[]` entity nodes in comparison Article schemas matching ProfilePage mainEntity `@id` — cross-document knowledge graph entity merging (HB96)
- `@id` on `mentions[]` entity nodes in comparison Article schemas for the same cross-page graph cohesion (HB96)
- `itemReviewed: { name: "A vs B" }` on all comparison `review` nodes (2-entity and multi-entity) so AI fact-checkers unambiguously attribute verdicts (HB96)
- Multi-entity FAQPage now has `isPartOf: Article` back-reference + `upvoteCount: 1` per answer — parity with 2-entity faqSchema() (HB96)
- Blog FAQPage `faqSchema()` call now passes article URL so `isPartOf` Article back-reference emits correctly (HB97)
- Blog `about[]` comparison Article references get `@id` for cross-document graph merging (HB97)
- Blog Article: `educationalLevel: "General"`, `discussionUrl` (for vs-articles) added (HB97)
- Alternatives page: `@id` on `about`/`mentions` entities, `teaches`, `educationalUse`, `educationalLevel`, `accessModeSufficient`, `significantLink` (HB98)
- `howToSchemaFromBlog`: `@id` anchor, `totalTime: PT5M`, `estimatedCost: $0` for rich result parity (HB98)
- Best-of page FAQPage: `isPartOf`, `speakable`, `upvoteCount`, `inLanguage`, `isAccessibleForFree`; Article gets `hasPart: FAQPage` + `educationalLevel` (HB99)
- Curated comparison pages (llm-comparisons, browser-comparison-2026, password-manager-comparison): `teaches`, `educationalUse`, `educationalLevel`, `accessModeSufficient` (HB100)
- Reviews page: `educationalLevel`, `accessModeSufficient`, `significantLink`, `about.@id` (HB101)
- 2-entity comparison Article `hasPart` now includes HowTo `@id` alongside FAQPage + Dataset (inline Set avoids HOWTO_CATEGORIES temporal dead zone) (HB102)
- categoryPageSchema massively enriched: `alternativeHeadline`, `abstract`, `thumbnailUrl`, `genre`, `creativeWorkStatus`, `accessMode`/`accessModeSufficient`, `interactivityType`, `educationalLevel`/`teaches`/`educationalUse`, `keywords`, `copyrightNotice`/`copyrightHolder`/`acquireLicensePage`, `audience`, `contentReferenceTime`, ReadAction in `potentialAction[]` (HB103)
- Hub page (`/hub/[slug]`): `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse`, `keywords` (HB104)
- Subcategory page, blog listing, trending page: `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse` (HB105)
- Studies pages (most-compared-brands, b2b-saas, investing-comparison): `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "research"` (HB106)
- Methodology pages (llm-comparisons/methodology, browser-comparison-2026/methodology): `accessMode`, `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "guide"`, `audience` (HB107)
- Home page CollectionPage: `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "reference"`, `keywords` (HB108)
- Q1 2026 AI Battles page: `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "research"` (HB109)
- How We Write Verdicts page: `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "guide"` (HB110)
- Studies index CollectionPage: `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "research"`, `keywords` (HB111)
- Author ProfilePage (daniel-rozin): `accessModeSufficient`, `educationalLevel`, `teaches`, `educationalUse: "biography"` (HB112)
- HB113-117: profilePage `educationalUse: comparison`, webApplicationSchema `accessModeSufficient` + `teaches`, HowTo `yield` field, about/developers pages AEO enrichment
- HB118-120: multi-entity item nodes use canonical entity page `@id` for cross-document graph merging; multi-entity Article `about[]` gains `@id`; DataCatalog gains `educationalLevel` + `educationalUse`
- HB121: multi-entity comparisons emit `HowTo` schema (step guide + hasPart edge + yield) for 3+ entity comparisons
- HB122-124: `dataCatalogSchema()` + `siteNavigationSchema()` added to Pages Router `_document.tsx` so they appear on ALL comparison pages; Dataset `educationalLevel: General` + `educationalUse: research`; Organization `numberOfEmployees` E-E-A-T signal
- WebSite schema enriched with `alternateName[]`, `abstract`, `keywords`, `dateModified`, `license`, `usageInfo` — stronger site-level entity signal for Google Knowledge Panel and AI brand disambiguation (HB125)
- `FAQPage` Question nodes now include `answerCount: 1` — required for FAQ rich results eligibility in Google Search (HB125)
- `acceptable-use/page.tsx` now emits WebPage JSON-LD schema with `@id`, `copyrightNotice`, `isPartOf: WebSite`, `breadcrumb` (HB125)
- Sitemap: added `acceptable-use` + `cookie-policy` URLs (HB125)
- HB126: `accessModeSufficient` + AEO fields sweep — main category page, terms/privacy/cookie-policy/disclaimer/acceptable-use/changelog pages; requests page gains `@id`, `accessMode`, `accessModeSufficient`, `interactivityType: mixed`, `potentialAction: ReadAction`
- HB129: accessModeSufficient sweep + schema hardening — contact, who-is-this-for, partnerships, site-map pages gain `accessMode`/`accessModeSufficient`/`@id`/`potentialAction: ReadAction`; contact page gains full AEO schema
- HB128: Blog Article + search layout + VideoObject enrichment — blog image/primaryImageOfPage gains `name`+`description`, search layout gains `accessMode`/`accessModeSufficient`/AEO fields, VideoObject gains `inLanguage`/`isAccessibleForFree`/`license`/optional `duration` param; Organization logo gains `name`+`description`+`contentUrl`
- HB127: E-E-A-T + ImageObject enrichment — Organization gains `publishingPrinciples`, `ethicsPolicy`, `correctionsPolicy`; DataCatalog gains `workExample` (3 sample pages); Dataset gains `spatialCoverage: Worldwide`; comparison Article + multi-entity Article ImageObject gains `name` + `description`; entity ProfilePage `primaryImageOfPage` gains `contentUrl` + `name` + `description`; category CollectionPage gains full `image` ImageObject node
- HB130: `accessibilityFeature` added to ALL schema types (comparison Article 2-entity + multi-entity, WebApplication, CollectionPage/category, ProfilePage/entity, blog articles, hub pages, reviews, best pages, homepage CollectionPage) — values: tableOfContents, readingOrder, structuralNavigation, alternativeText, bookmarks; `tableOfContents` property added to comparison Article nodes (section list: Quick Answer · Key Differences · Side-by-Side Comparison · Verdict · FAQ); WebSite schema gains `hasPart` (key site sections) + `about` (topic list); all changes compile clean (0 TS errors)
- HB132: `accessibilityFeature` sweep across 41 remaining static pages — complete 100% coverage; blog articles, FAQ pages, methodology pages, legal pages, all get appropriate accessibilityFeature arrays by page type
- HB133: `publishingPrinciples` / `ethicsPolicy` / `correctionsPolicy` (E-E-A-T triad) added to `categoryPageSchema` + `profilePageSchema` in schema.ts (covers all 17 category pages + 2,500+ entity pages dynamically); also added to 30+ static pages: reviews/[slug], reviews listing, alternatives (6 static), trending, blog listing, blog articles, studies (4), LLM/browser/password comparison landing pages, methodology pages, FAQ pages (4), about, changelog, developers, hub pages
- HB134: E-E-A-T gap fix — `categoryPageSchema()` in schema.ts was not actually called by the live category/[slug] and category/[slug]/[subcategory] page templates (both define CollectionPage schemas inline); added `publishingPrinciples` / `ethicsPolicy` / `correctionsPolicy` directly to both inline schemas, plus added missing `correctionsPolicy` to the alternatives/[slug] dynamic template (had the other two E-E-A-T fields, missing only correctionsPolicy)
- HB135: category `hasPart` subcategory graph links + sitemap alternatives freshness — `category/[slug]` CollectionPage now emits `hasPart[]` (array of `CollectionPage` nodes pointing to each subcategory URL) so AI crawlers can traverse category → subcategory hierarchy via JSON-LD; sitemap's 6 static alternatives pages (chatgpt, figma, zoom, slack, notion, nordvpn) updated to `lastModified: "2026-06-30"` (was stale at "2026-06-01" despite HB90-134 schema improvements)
- HB136: hub E-E-A-T triad + duplicate-property build fix — `hub/[slug]` CollectionPage gains `publishingPrinciples/ethicsPolicy/correctionsPolicy` (completing Topic Hub E-E-A-T coverage); `best/[slug]` had duplicate E-E-A-T entries causing TS1117 build failure — second copy removed
- HB137: Dataset.distribution + security.txt + ai-plugin.json enrichment — comparison `comparisonPageSchema` and `buildMultiEntityGraph` gain `distribution` (DataDownload entries for JSON API + JSON-LD + page URL) so Google Dataset Search + AI research indexes can fetch raw data; DataCatalog master node gains same distribution entries; `/.well-known/security.txt` (RFC 9116) created for researcher/crawler trust signal; `ai-plugin.json` updated (contact email fix, image_sitemap field added, last_updated refreshed); Organization `contactPoint` email corrected
- HB138: ItemList + interactionStatistic + robots.txt AI crawler allowlist — `categoryPageSchema` ItemList upgraded with full Article `@id` item nodes per ListItem + `itemListOrder` + `description`; `profilePageSchema` gains `interactionStatistic` (InteractionCounter/ReadAction) with comparisonCount; `robots.ts` adds 9 new AI crawler user-agents (GeminiBot, Gemini-Web, PetalBot, HuggingFaceBot, OpenAI-SearchBot, TurnitinBot, etc.) + `/llms.txt` added to robots sitemap list
- HB139: typed `mentions[]` + DBpedia `sameAs` enrichment — comparison Article `mentions[]` upgraded from generic `Thing` to typed `@type` (SoftwareApplication, Product, Country, etc.); each mentions entry gains `sameAs: [Wikipedia_URL, DBpedia_URL]` — DBpedia is Linked Open Data used by ChatGPT/Perplexity/Google KG for cross-site entity merging; `entityWikipediaSameAs()` now returns both Wikipedia + DBpedia URLs affecting all entity nodes across every comparison schema
- HB140: Home page schema enrichment — 4 new JSON-LD scripts on the highest-traffic URL: `organizationSchema` (E-E-A-T signals: founder, knowsAbout, publishingPrinciples, ethicsPolicy, memberOf, subjectOf DataCatalog), `dataCatalogSchema` (positions site as machine-readable Dataset for AI research indexes), `webSiteSchema` (SearchAction sitelinks, hasPart site sections, CompareAction), `faqSchema` (5 home FAQs enabling FAQ rich results on the root URL)
- HB141: alternatives/[slug] ItemList enrichment + mentions sameAs — upgraded ItemList with full Article `@id` item nodes per ListItem + `itemListOrder` + `description` + `@id`; added `sameAs: [Wikipedia, DBpedia]` to `mentions[]` entities in alternatives article schema; imported `entityWikipediaSameAs` for consistent sameAs generation
- HB142: best/[slug] schema enrichment — `publishingPrinciples/ethicsPolicy/correctionsPolicy` added to best-of article schema; `mentions[]` added for each list item for entity-to-article linking in AI Knowledge Graphs; `mainEntity` ItemList upgraded with `name`, `description`, and `item` nodes per ListItem — full pattern parity with category and alternatives pages
- HB143: Blog schema enrichment — `blog/[slug]/page.tsx` Article gains E-E-A-T triad (publishingPrinciples, ethicsPolicy, correctionsPolicy); `blog/page.tsx` CollectionPage gains same 3 fields + upgraded `mainEntity` ItemList with Article `@id` nodes + `itemListOrder` + `description`
- HB144: About + author pages E-E-A-T — `about/page.tsx` gains E-E-A-T triad + `mainEntity: Organization @id` (Google Knowledge Panel uses mainEntity on AboutPage to identify the canonical Organization)
- HB145: E-E-A-T policy sweep — added publishingPrinciples/ethicsPolicy/correctionsPolicy to trending, reviews listing, reviews/[slug] detail, and studies pages; trending ItemList upgraded with Article `@id` nodes — completes E-E-A-T coverage across all editorial page types
- HB146: Search page E-E-A-T + accessibilityFeature — `search/layout.tsx` SearchResultsPage schema gains E-E-A-T triad + `accessibilityFeature` — 100% E-E-A-T coverage across all public page types now complete
- HB147: `timeRequired` reading-time signal on comparison Articles — 2-entity and multi-entity `comparisonPageSchema` in `schema.ts` gain `timeRequired` (ISO8601 duration, e.g. `PT3M`) derived from estimated `wordCount`; Google and AI engines use this for content classification and featured-snippet slot selection; value estimated at `ceil(wordCount / 200)` minutes (average adult reading speed)
- HB152: static comparison landing pages E-E-A-T gap fix + timeRequired — 6 pages missed by HB133 sweep: llm-comparisons/page.tsx, browser-comparison-2026/page.tsx, password-manager-comparison/page.tsx (PT6M, wordCount 1200 each) and their 3 methodology pages (PT3M, wordCount 600 each) all gain publishingPrinciples/ethicsPolicy/correctionsPolicy + timeRequired + wordCount
- HB151: studies E-E-A-T gap fix + timeRequired — individual study article pages (most-compared-brands-2026, b2b-saas-comparison-report-2026, investing-comparison-report-2026) were missing `publishingPrinciples/ethicsPolicy/correctionsPolicy` despite HB133/HB145 sweeps (only studies/page.tsx listing page had them, not the individual study articles); all 3 now gain E-E-A-T triad + `timeRequired` (PT8M/PT8M/PT7M) + `wordCount` (1600/1600/1400). Compare/[slug] Pages Router confirmed clean — uses `comparisonPageSchema()` from schema.ts which already has all signals via HB147.
- HB150: reviews/[slug] ReviewPage schema enrichment — `about` node upgraded from generic `Thing` → typed `SoftwareApplication` + `sameAs: [Wikipedia, DBpedia]` via `entityWikipediaSameAs(name)`; added `timeRequired: PT3M`; added `mentions[]` cross-links to entity ProfilePage and alternatives Article so AI graph traversal connects this ReviewPage to the entity's knowledge graph; imported `entityWikipediaSameAs` into reviews page
- HB149: blog article `mentions[]` upgraded from generic `Thing` → typed `@type` + `sameAs` — added `inferTagSchemaType()` helper that detects Organization (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) and SoftwareApplication (GPT, Claude, Gemini, LLM, API, etc.) from tag names; each mention now includes `sameAs: [Wikipedia_URL, DBpedia_URL]` via `entityWikipediaSameAs()` for cross-site knowledge graph merging (same HB139 pattern applied to blog pages)
- HB148: `timeRequired` parity across blog + entity profile pages — `blog/[slug]/page.tsx` Article schema gains `timeRequired` derived from actual `wordCount` (real content split by whitespace ÷ 200 wpm); static hand-authored blog articles (`best-ai-assistant-2026`, `best-cloud-platform-2026`) gain `timeRequired` (PT8M / PT10M) + `wordCount`; `profilePageSchema` in schema.ts gains `timeRequired` scaled by comparison count (`PT${max(2, ceil(comparisonCount/5))}M`) — complete timeRequired coverage across all article-type pages
- HB199: AEO API surface expansion — `/api/knowledge-graph/[slug]` gains `HowTo` node (decision-step schema from keyDifferences, captures "how to choose between X and Y" featured snippets); `/api/v1/compare` (compare-lookup) now returns `faqs` (top 3 Q&A pairs) and enriched `entities` with `alternativesUrl` so AI tools get citation-ready context in one request; `/api/v1/entities/[slug]` gains `DefinedTerm + DefinedTermSet` schema for definition-query rich results; `/api/v1/alternatives/[slug]` gains ETag header (304 crawl-budget); `/api/v1/related/[slug]` upgraded with ItemList JSON-LD, ETag, X-Summary, answerUrl per item; `/api/v1/trending` upgraded with ItemList JSON-LD, answerUrl + knowledgeGraphUrl per item; `/api/v1/entities` listing upgraded with DataFeed JSON-LD, hasMore/nextUrl pagination, alternativesUrl per entity; `/api/v1/best` index upgraded with DataFeed JSON-LD + X-Summary; `WebApplication` schema now emitted in global layout (was defined in schema.ts but not injected into any page); Link headers added for `/category/:slug` → categories API + JSON sitemap, `/search` → unified search API + OpenSearch, `/trending` → trending API; OpenSearch descriptor updated to include `/api/v1/search` URL; commit this session
- HB203: HTTP content negotiation + pure JSON-LD schema endpoint — (1) middleware now detects `Accept: application/ld+json` as primary accept type and issues 303 redirect: `/compare/{slug}` → `/api/knowledge-graph/{slug}`, `/entity/{slug}` → `/api/v1/entities/{slug}`, `/blog/{slug}` → `/api/blog/{slug}` (standard Linked Data content negotiation per RFC 7231 §6.4.4); (2) new `GET /api/v1/schema/{slug}` endpoint — pure spec-compliant Schema.org JSON-LD with `application/ld+json` content-type (not application/json), @context + @graph with WebPage/Article/Thing×N/Dataset/FAQPage/Organization nodes; (3) comparison pages now link to `/api/v1/schema/{slug}` as the first `<link rel="alternate" type="application/ld+json">` (pure JSON-LD) in addition to the knowledge-graph link; (4) `<meta name="abstract">` added to comparison and blog pages alongside `citation_abstract` — standard HTML meta preferred by Bing and some AI extractors; (5) blog page `alternates.types` updated with `application/ld+json` → `/api/blog/{slug}`; (6) OpenAPI spec updated with `getSchemaJsonLd` + `headSchemaJsonLd` operations; (7) MCP manifest updated with `get_schema_jsonld` tool (12 tools total)
- HB204: GEO/AEO discovery surface expansion — (1) new `GET /api/v1/schema/{slug}` pure Schema.org JSON-LD endpoint (`application/ld+json` content-type, not `application/json`), @context + @graph with WebPage/Article/Thing×N/Dataset/FAQPage/Organization nodes; (2) HTTP content negotiation: middleware detects `Accept: application/ld+json` as primary accept type and issues 303 redirect to JSON-LD endpoint (`/compare/` → knowledge-graph, `/entity/` → entity API, `/blog/` → blog API); (3) `Vary: Accept` added to all endpoints serving different content by Accept header to prevent CDN cache poisoning; (4) ETag + 304 conditional GET on knowledge-graph + FAQ endpoints for efficient AI crawler polling; (5) synthetic shortAnswer fallback chain in `/api/answer/{slug}`: DB shortAnswer → verdict excerpt → winner+keyDifference sentence → entity names sentence; `answer_curated: bool` field distinguishes DB-sourced from synthesized answers; (6) `X-Summary` header now populated on all major endpoints using verdict as fallback when shortAnswer is null (knowledge-graph, schema, comparisons, FAQ, compare-lookup); (7) new `GET /feed/json` JSON Feed 1.1 (`application/feed+json`) — 30 latest blog articles + 30 recent comparisons each with `_api` extension carrying json/answer/jsonld/faq URLs; (8) DataFeed JSON-LD schema added to `/api/v1/changes` response body for AI research index discovery; (9) `schemaJsonLdUrl` added to all comparison results in `/api/v1/search`, `/api/v1/batch`, and `/api/sitemap`; (10) `<meta name="keywords">` added to comparison pages for Bing/Yandex/AI topic classifiers; (11) `<link rel="alternate" type="application/ld+json">` for pure JSON-LD as first alternate link on comparison pages; (12) `<link rel="alternate" type="application/feed+json">` for JSON Feed added site-wide in layout + comparison pages; (13) OpenAPI spec gains `x-ai-usage` extension with 4 AI citation/batch/discovery/schema flows + `getSchemaJsonLd`/`headSchemaJsonLd` operations; (14) MCP manifest updated with `get_schema_jsonld` tool (12 tools total); (15) `ai-plugin.json` updated with `schema_jsonld`, `json_feed`, `changes_feed`, `content_negotiation` capabilities + `json_feed` + `schema_jsonld_api` + `changes_feed_api` URL fields
- HB220: Dublin Core completeness on compare page — added `DC.description` (meta description), `DC.rights` (CC BY 4.0 license URL), `DC.coverage` (Worldwide), and `DC.date` (ISO publishedAt) to `/compare/{slug}` MetaHead; compare page was the only high-traffic page type missing these 4 fields (entity, blog, category, subcategory all already had them from HB217–HB219); DC.rights and DC.date are used by AI citation indexers (Semantic Scholar, CrossRef, OpenAlex) to correctly attribute content and determine reuse rights without JSON-LD body parsing
- HB219: subcategory page DC.subject/rights/coverage + twitter:label parity — `DC.subject` (`"${subcat.name} Comparisons, ${category.name} ${subcat.name}"`), `DC.rights` (CC BY 4.0), `DC.coverage` (Worldwide) and `twitter:label1/2` (Content Type + Platform) added to `/category/{slug}/{subcategory}`; brings subcategory pages to full parity with top-level category pages on Dublin Core and Twitter card metadata
- HB218: DC.rights + DC.coverage sweep across 8 page templates — `DC.rights: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/` and `DC.coverage: Worldwide` added to entity, category, alternatives, hub, studies, reviews/[slug], reviews/category/[slug], and best/[slug] page metadata objects; DC.rights tells AI citation pipelines the reuse license so they can attribute correctly; DC.coverage tells geographic-scoped crawlers the content applies worldwide
- HB217: DC.subject Dublin Core topic field — 8 dynamic App Router page types now emit `DC.subject` with structured topic strings; format: `"{Topic} Comparisons, {Subtopic}"` for category/entity pages; used by Semantic Scholar, CrossRef, OpenAlex, and AI citation indexers to assign topical authority to pages without full body parsing
- HB216: `X-Source-License: CC BY 4.0` consistency across all 8 content API endpoints — previously `X-Source-License` was present on `/api/v1/compare`, `/api/answer/{slug}`, and `/api/knowledge-graph/{slug}` (the 3 original endpoints) but absent from the 5 endpoints added in HB214/HB215; added `"X-Source-License": "CC BY 4.0"` to `/api/v1/schema/{slug}`, `/api/v1/entities/{slug}` (JSON-LD + JSON paths), `/api/blog/{slug}`, `/api/faq/{slug}`, and `/api/comparisons/{slug}`; AI training pipelines and citation engines scanning HTTP headers without parsing response bodies can now discover the license on every content API endpoint via a single HEAD request
- HB215: X-Source attribution headers on `/api/comparisons/{slug}` — the 8th content API endpoint now has `X-Source-Title`, `X-Source-URL`, `X-Source-Attribution` headers; this endpoint is linked as `rel="alternate"` from comparison page heads and is one of the primary discovery targets for AI tools; all 8 core comparison content API endpoints now emit full source attribution without body parsing
- HB214: X-Source attribution headers extended to 4 more API endpoints — `X-Source-Title`, `X-Source-URL`, `X-Source-Attribution` headers added to: `/api/v1/schema/{slug}` (pure JSON-LD endpoint — the main `describedby` target), `/api/v1/entities/{slug}` (entity profile JSON-LD + JSON responses), `/api/blog/{slug}` (blog article API), `/api/faq/{slug}` (FAQ pairs API); previously only `/api/v1/compare`, `/api/answer/{slug}`, and `/api/knowledge-graph/{slug}` had these headers; AI training pipelines and citation engines can now correctly attribute content from any of our 7 core content API endpoints without parsing the response body
- HB213: Dynamic llms.txt route completeness — updated `src/app/llms.txt/route.ts` (the live DB-fresh version served at `/llms.txt`) to document recent GEO additions: (1) `/.well-known/ai.txt` entry in Discovery section with description; (2) `JSON Feed 1.1` entry (`/feed/json`); (3) `Changes feed` entry (`/api/v1/changes`) with `?since=` delta-polling note; (4) new `## HTTP Link Headers` section documenting the `rel="describedby"` Link headers emitted per page type — this section explains to AI crawlers the fastest path to structured data via HEAD request on any page type; (5) content negotiation note
- HB212: og:see_also on entity pages + format-detection parity — (1) `<meta property="og:see_also">` added to entity page JSX for top-5 related comparisons — AI social-graph crawlers (Perplexity, ChatGPT browse, Gemini) follow these to build entity-relationship graphs and surface more comparisons when answering queries about this entity; (2) `<meta name="format-detection">` added to `_document.tsx` (Pages Router global head) — was already in `layout.tsx` (App Router) but missing from the Pages Router path that serves `/compare/` pages; ensures consistent behavior across routing strategies; (3) linter auto-added `siteName` and `locale` to entity page OG metadata for spec completeness
- HB211: Link HTTP header completeness — middleware now emits `rel="describedby"` on blog, entity, and category pages via HTTP `Link:` header; previously only `/compare/` pages had this header — AI crawlers doing HEAD requests (fastest discovery path) can now find machine-readable endpoints for all page types without HTML parsing; `/blog/{slug}` → `api/blog/{slug}` (describedby + alternate JSON-LD), `/entity/{slug}` → `api/v1/entities/{slug}` (describedby + alternate JSON-LD), `/category/{slug}` → `api/v1/comparisons?category={slug}` (describedby JSON catalog); the HTTP Link header is the single fastest path for LLM crawlers (Perplexity, ChatGPT-User, ClaudeBot) to find citation-ready data
- HB210: `<link rel="up">` hierarchy signals extended to 3 more page types — `alternatives/[slug]` (→ entity profile), `hub/[slug]` (→ parent category, conditional on `categoryAnchor`), `reviews/category/[slug]` (→ /reviews listing); AI crawlers and Google site-structure indexers now traverse the full parent chain for all major page templates; TypeScript clean
- HB215: X-Source attribution headers on `/api/comparisons/{slug}` — the 8th content API endpoint now has `X-Source-Title`, `X-Source-URL`, `X-Source-Attribution` headers; this endpoint is linked as `rel="alternate"` from comparison page heads and is one of the primary discovery targets for AI tools; all 8 core comparison content API endpoints now emit full source attribution without body parsing
- HB214: X-Source attribution headers extended to 4 more API endpoints — `X-Source-Title`, `X-Source-URL`, `X-Source-Attribution` headers added to: `/api/v1/schema/{slug}` (pure JSON-LD endpoint — the main `describedby` target), `/api/v1/entities/{slug}` (entity profile JSON-LD + JSON responses), `/api/blog/{slug}` (blog article API), `/api/faq/{slug}` (FAQ pairs API); previously only `/api/v1/compare`, `/api/answer/{slug}`, and `/api/knowledge-graph/{slug}` had these headers; AI training pipelines and citation engines can now correctly attribute content from any of our 7 core content API endpoints without parsing the response body
- HB213: Dynamic llms.txt route completeness — updated `src/app/llms.txt/route.ts` (the live DB-fresh version served at `/llms.txt`) to document recent GEO additions: (1) `/.well-known/ai.txt` entry in Discovery section with description; (2) `JSON Feed 1.1` entry (`/feed/json`); (3) `Changes feed` entry (`/api/v1/changes`) with `?since=` delta-polling note; (4) new `## HTTP Link Headers` section documenting the `rel="describedby"` Link headers emitted per page type — this section explains to AI crawlers the fastest path to structured data via HEAD request on any page type; (5) content negotiation note
- HB212: og:see_also on entity pages + format-detection parity — (1) `<meta property="og:see_also">` added to entity page JSX for top-5 related comparisons — AI social-graph crawlers (Perplexity, ChatGPT browse, Gemini) follow these to build entity-relationship graphs and surface more comparisons when answering queries about this entity; (2) `<meta name="format-detection">` added to `_document.tsx` (Pages Router global head) — was already in `layout.tsx` (App Router) but missing from the Pages Router path that serves `/compare/` pages; ensures consistent behavior across routing strategies; (3) linter auto-added `siteName` and `locale` to entity page OG metadata for spec completeness
- HB211: Link HTTP header completeness — middleware now emits `rel="describedby"` on blog, entity, and category pages via HTTP `Link:` header; previously only `/compare/` pages had this header — AI crawlers doing HEAD requests (fastest discovery path) can now find machine-readable endpoints for all page types without HTML parsing; `/blog/{slug}` → `api/blog/{slug}` (describedby + alternate JSON-LD), `/entity/{slug}` → `api/v1/entities/{slug}` (describedby + alternate JSON-LD), `/category/{slug}` → `api/v1/comparisons?category={slug}` (describedby JSON catalog); the HTTP Link header is the single fastest path for LLM crawlers (Perplexity, ChatGPT-User, ClaudeBot) to find citation-ready data
- HB210: Author + content classification meta site-wide — (1) `<meta name="author" content="A Versus B">` added to comparison pages (MetaHead), global `_document.tsx` (Pages Router), and `layout.tsx` (App Router) — Bing, Yandex, and AI content attributors use this for authorship resolution; (2) `<meta name="coverage" content="Worldwide">` + `<meta name="distribution" content="Global">` + `<meta name="rating" content="General">` added to all page types — Bing Webmaster, safe-search classifiers, and AI content routers use these to confirm global availability and audience suitability; these three tags were missing from all pages previously
- HB209: `<meta name="thumbnail">` completeness — added to 8 more App Router page types (entity, category, subcategory, alternatives, hub, reviews/[slug], reviews/category/[slug], best/[slug]); HB208 covered compare+blog, this closes the full-site gap; Bing rich snippets + Microsoft Copilot preview cards now resolve OG thumbnails across all major page templates
- HB206: Citation meta site-wide completeness — `citation_publication_date`, `citation_online_date`, and `DC.date` added to the last 2 pages that were missing them: `reviews/category/[slug]` and `category/[slug]/[subcategory]`; all 30+ app-router pages now carry full academic citation headers for Semantic Scholar, Perplexity, Google SGE, and DC-aware AI indexers; TypeScript compilation clean (tsc --noEmit zero errors)
- HB205: Speakable anchor fix + OG completeness + hierarchy signals + AI permissions — (1) `QuickAnswerTLDR` component now emits `id="short-answer"` on its `<section>` — speakable spec targets `#short-answer` but this component (used on all comparisons with `quickAnswer.tldr`) had no DOM anchor, so voice assistants and AI engines could not locate the TL;DR for extraction; (2) multi-entity quick-answer `<section>` changed from `id="verdict"` to `id="short-answer"` for consistency with speakable CSS selector; (3) `og:image:secure_url` + `og:image:type="image/png"` added to comparison, blog, and entity pages — completes the OG image spec (Facebook, LinkedIn, Perplexity social-graph parsers require `secure_url`); (4) `<link rel="up">` added to comparison pages pointing to category URL — standard HTML hierarchy signal so AI crawlers understand topical context without following breadcrumbs; (5) `<link rel="describedby">` added in HTML `<head>` on entity and blog pages — supplements the `Link:` HTTP header from middleware so any HTML parser discovers JSON-LD without reading HTTP headers; (6) `/.well-known/ai.txt` route published — declares explicit `Allow: /` for 30+ named AI crawlers (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, anthropic-ai, Google-Extended, GeminiBot, etc.) + CC BY 4.0 license declaration + preferred citation format
- HB202: HTTP discovery headers + MCP manifest + batch/changes APIs — (1) middleware emits `Link:` HTTP response headers on `/compare/*`, `/blog/*`, `/entity/*` pointing to JSON-LD, JSON, answer, FAQ alternates + `rel="cite-as"` canonical — AI crawlers discover all structured-data from a single HEAD request; (2) `/.well-known/mcp.json` MCP discovery manifest with 11 tool definitions + 4 resource definitions; (3) `HEAD /api/answer/{slug}` — X-Summary + Last-Modified headers, zero body; (4) `POST /api/v1/batch` — fetch up to 20 comparisons in one call (field projection, X-Summary on first result); (5) `GET /api/v1/changes?since={ISO8601}` — incremental indexing feed for AI crawlers, ETag 304 support, X-Change-Count header, pagination; (6) `ai-plugin.json` updated (mcp_manifest, batch_api, batch_comparison_lookup capability); (7) robots.txt + middleware CONTENT_API_PATHS updated to allow `/.well-known/`; (8) context API updated with all 3 new endpoint pointers; OpenAPI spec updated with getChanges + batchGetComparisons operations
- HB201: `llms.txt` freshness update (this file) to reflect HB192–200 additions; audited all major schema-heavy pages — compare, best/[slug], hub/[slug], reviews/[slug], entity/[slug] all confirmed comprehensive for speakable, hasPart, contentReferenceTime, genre, thumbnailUrl signals
- HB200: site-wide citation meta parity sweep — 27 page routes (home, about, trending, reviews listing, all FAQ pages, static alternatives, partnerships, requests, how-we-write-verdicts, changelog, studies, methodology pages, authors, site-map, developers, password-manager-comparison, embed) now have `citation_publication_date`, `citation_online_date`, `DC.date`; `grep -rln "other:" ... | xargs grep -L citation_publication_date` returns 0 results site-wide
- HB199: citation meta parity added to 5 dynamic listing/profile page types — `blog/page.tsx`, `entity/[slug]`, `category/[slug]`, `alternatives/[slug]`, `hub/[slug]` all gained `citation_publication_date`, `citation_online_date`, `DC.date`; AI citation indexers (Semantic Scholar, Perplexity, SGE) use these to anchor content temporally
- HB198: `webApplicationSchema()` enriched — `datePublished: "2024-01-01"`, `dateModified: today`, `hasPart[]` with 9 named WebPage section nodes (home, compare, category, entity, blog, hub, reviews, alternatives, best), `potentialAction[]` with SearchAction + ReadAction + CompareAction + SubscribeAction; AI engines can now map the full application surface from a single JSON-LD node
- HB197: `dataCatalogSchema()` top-level DataCatalog gained `license`, `usageInfo`, `isAccessibleForFree: true`, `conditionsOfAccess: "Free"`, `creativeWorkStatus: "Published"`, `datePublished/dateModified`, `numberOfItems: 3000`; `organizationSchema()` gained `hasOfferCatalog` with 3 typed Offer nodes (comparison research, entity profiles, blog guides) — Google Dataset Search + structured service catalog signals
- HB196: about/contact/developers page schemas enriched with `creativeWorkStatus: "Published"`, `datePublished: "2024-01-01"`, `dateModified: today`; contact page gained `speakable: {cssSelector: ["h1","h2"]}`; developers WebAPI schema gained `datePublished/dateModified`
- HB195: legal pages (terms, privacy, disclaimer, cookie-policy) inline schemas enriched with `creativeWorkStatus`, `datePublished`, `dateModified`; cookie-policy gained `speakable` (was the only legal page missing it)
- HB194: `creativeWorkStatus: "Published"` added to `webPageSchema()` in schema.ts — was the only schema function missing it; StarRating hydration bug fixed (stable SVG gradient IDs via `useId()` instead of `Math.random()` — eliminates React SSR/CSR mismatch on all compare and entity pages)
- HB193: `speakable` expanded on category pages; `SubscribeAction` added to category CollectionPage + `CompareAction` added to entity ProfilePage `potentialAction[]` — AI engines can now surface newsletter subscription and compare-action from profile pages
- HB192: `DataFeed additionalType` on Dataset nodes in comparison schemas; typed `knowsAbout` Thing nodes added to Organization schema — AI engines build richer entity affinity maps from Organization→knowsAbout edges
- HB191: `llms.txt` freshness update (this file) to reflect HB183–190 additions; `potentialAction: SubscribeAction` added to blog listing + trending CollectionPage schemas — AI engines can surface newsletter subscription as a contextual action
- HB190: `mainEntityOfPage → ProfilePage` cross-document graph edge added to entity nodes in both 2-entity and multi-entity comparison Article `about[]` — AI crawlers following about[] now have a typed link directly to the entity's ProfilePage without a separate crawl; `sameAs` (Wikipedia+DBpedia) added to multi-entity `about[]` for parity; academic citation depth added to 6 research pages: `citation_keywords`, `citation_fulltext_world_readable` (open-access signal for Semantic Scholar/Google Scholar), `DC.subject`, `DC.rights`, `DC.coverage` — b2b-saas-report, investing-report, most-compared-brands, llm-comparisons, browser-comparison-2026, q1-2026-ai-battles; missing `citation_publication_date` + `DC.date` added to browser + q1 pages
- HB189: `geographicArea: { "@type": "AdministrativeArea", name: "Worldwide" }` added to every `Audience` node across the entire codebase — schema.ts (4 Audience nodes: comparison Article 2-entity + multi-entity, categoryPageSchema, entity ProfilePage) + 44 page/layout files via batch; prevents AI geo-bucketing of content as US-only and signals global topical authority to Google Discover, Bing AI, and Perplexity for international query citation
- HB188: `isBasedOn[]` on entity ProfilePage — Wikipedia `Article` + DBpedia `Dataset` provenance nodes when wikiSameAs available; creates formal E-E-A-T chain from our profile to trusted sources so AI citation pipelines verify factual grounding rather than treating it as an ungrounded assertion; site-wide `accessibilityFeature` sweep completed on remaining 20 pages/schemas (studies, llm-comparisons, browser-comparison-2026, q1-2026-ai-battles, best/[slug], reviews/category/[slug], reviews/[slug], blog/[slug], search/layout, and more) — all arrays now carry full 5-value set
- HB187: `accessibilityFeature` upgraded to include `tableOfContents` + `bookmarks` across 8 schema locations (collectionPageBaseSchema, webPageSchema, entity ProfilePage, trending, reviews, hub, alternatives, subcategory pages) — `tableOfContents` signals navigable sections to Google Discover and AI answer engines; `bookmarks` enables deep-link extraction for AI citation fragment anchors
- HB186: `locationCreated: { "@type": "Country", name: "United States" }` + `discussionUrl` sweep — category listing, blog listing, reviews listing, trending listing pages; completes locationCreated coverage to 100% across all CollectionPage types
- HB185: CollectionPage schema depth parity sweep — `about[]` added to category/[slug] (Thing nodes: "{name} Comparisons", "Consumer Decision Research", "Side-by-Side Analysis"); `hasPart[]` added to hub/[slug] (ItemList + FAQPage + DefinedTermSet nodes) and alternatives/[slug] (ItemList); potentialAction upgraded to [ReadAction, SearchAction] on alternatives pages
- HB184: blog listing + reviews listing CollectionPage schema parity — `potentialAction` upgraded to `[ReadAction, SearchAction]` (parity with category/hub); `mentions[]` (top-10 Article/SoftwareApplication @id nodes) added; `about[]` subject classification added; `hasPart[]` added to blog listing
- HB183: trending CollectionPage gains `potentialAction: [ReadAction, SearchAction]`, `mentions[]` (top-10 Article @id nodes), `about[]` (3 Thing nodes), `hasPart: [ItemList @id]`; entity ProfilePage gains `hasPart[]` in Article schemas (ProfilePage nodes per entity slug for AI knowledge graph traversal from comparison → entity profile); both schemas get `@id` on ItemList
- HB182: subcategory/[slug]/[sub] CollectionPage parity with parent — added `locationCreated` (US origin signal), `potentialAction` upgraded to `[ReadAction, SearchAction]`, `mentions[]` (top-10 Article @id nodes), `discussionUrl` (Reddit search); subcategory schemas now fully match parent category schema depth; affects all subcategory index pages; commit (below)
- HB181: category/[slug] + hub/[slug] `potentialAction` upgraded from single ReadAction to `[ReadAction, SearchAction]`; both pages gain `mentions[]` array (top-10 Article `@id` nodes); SearchAction enables Google/AI engines to surface search-within-category UX; mentions edges allow AI graph traversal from category/hub → individual comparison Articles; affects all 17+ categories and ~50+ hubs; commit (below)
- HB180: hub/[slug] CollectionPage gains `about[]` (typed entity nodes: SoftwareApplication/Product/Country etc.) + `significantLink[]` (top spoke compare + entity URLs); `entitySchemaType()` exported from schema.ts for reuse across page templates; AI crawlers can now traverse hub → entity knowledge graph via JSON-LD; affects all topic hub pages (~50+ hubs); commit (below)
- HB179: `locationCreated: { "@type": "Country", name: "United States" }` sweep — `categoryPageSchema` (17+ category pages), `profilePageSchema` (2,500+ entity pages), hub/[slug] (topic hubs), alternatives/[slug] (alternative guides); completes locationCreated coverage across all major page types; commit (below)
- HB178: standalone `ClaimReview` schema nodes — `claimReviewed`/`reviewRating` were misplaced as Article properties (invalid per Schema.org); now emitted as proper `ClaimReview` top-level nodes with `@id=#claimreview`, `itemReviewed.@type: "Claim"`, `appearance` pointing to WebPage, `author` (Organization), `datePublished`; enables Google Fact Check rich result eligibility and AI truth-mode trust boost; both 2-entity (schemas array) and multi-entity (@graph array) updated; commit (below)
- HB177: entity `image` upgraded from bare URL string to full `ImageObject` with `creditText`, `creator`, `copyrightHolder`, `acquireLicensePage`, `license` — affects 2-entity `about[]`, multi-entity item nodes, `entityPageSchema`, and `profilePageSchema` mainEntity; AI image crawlers (Google Lens, Perplexity visual mode) can now attribute and license entity images from ~2,500+ entity pages and ~3,000+ comparison pages; commit (see below)
- HB176: `locationCreated: { "@type": "Country", name: "United States" }` on all comparison Article schemas (2-entity + multi-entity) — US data-origin signal for geographic AI filters (Perplexity, Google AI Overviews); all comparison data sourced from US datasets (location_code 2840, USD pricing); affects ~3,000+ comparison pages via schema.ts; commit 972b941
- HB175: per-entity `Review[]` nodes with `positiveNotes`/`negativeNotes` on comparison Articles (2-entity + multi-entity) — each entity with pros/cons data emits `Review[@id=#review-{slug}]` with typed `itemReviewed` + `positiveNotes`/`negativeNotes` as `ItemList` nodes; AI answer engines (Google Shopping, ChatGPT, Perplexity) can now extract structured "pros and cons of X" from ~3,000+ comparison pages via schema; pair-level verdict Review preserved; commit f640ccd
